intro: Gilia exilis, also known as the slender gilia, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Polemoniaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, gastrointestinal issues, and inflammation, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Gilia exilis include alkaloids, such as giliaflavine and giliaflavinone, as well as other compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. The most common herbal preparations made with Gilia exilis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms associated with colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
